Project_Description: PRISM is a pressure-sensing system for the interface between a transtibial prosthetic socket and the residual limb. It integrates a distributed array of sensors into a standard prosthetic sock. Calibrated to each user’s anatomy, PRISM measures localized pressure during daily use and presents the data as intuitive, real-time visual feedback. This helps users adjust sock plies throughout the day and enables clinicians to identify high-risk pressure regions, mitigate skin degradation, and make informed socket design decisions.
Team_Members: Joel Lee, Gloria So, Livia Murray, Gary Liu
